SPECIALISTS HOSPITAL SHREVEPORT
FINANCIAL POLICY
Thank you for choosing us as your health care provider. We are committed to providing the best treatment possible for our patients, and to your treatment
being successful. We hope that you understand that our credit and collection policies are a necessary part of assuring the financial resources needed to
maintain this vital health care facility for our patients and community.
Our charges for your care arte considered to be the usual and customary charges in line with what other hospitals in this geographical area charge their patients.
You are responsible for payment of your bill in full, regardless of your insurance company’s determination of usual and customary charges for this area. The
only exceptions for this are if you are covered by Medicare, or you are covered by a PPO or HMO for which we are a provider of services.
a li ET
STATEMENT OF RESPONSIBILITY
eee eee SSS SSS ee
This date, | have a contract with SPECIALISTS HOSPITAL SHREVEPORT (SHS), A Professional Medical Corporation, for the furnishing of medical and/or
surgical procedures for iltness or injury. 1 understand that I am responsible for the total bill incurred as a result of treatment received. I have been advised that
the surgeon, the anesthesia provider, and the pathologist will bill me separately for their services. Although I may have insurance coverage, I understand that
this is an agreement between me and my insurance carrier to pay certain amounts for my medical care. The SHS bill is an agreement between me and the
facility, and I am totally responsible for payment of this bill in full. This is regardless of the status of any pending insurance claim, and regardless of the
insurance company’s determination of usual and customary rates or amount of assignment. I accept full responsibility for payment of the account, and,
depending upon the circumstances, I may be expected to pay in full at time of service.
In the event that I am covered by a managed care PPO or HMO for which this facility is not a provider, 1 understand that Specialists Hospital Shreveport will
accept the allowable charges. I will not owe any amount that is disallowed by the insurance carrier. ] accept responsibility for payment of my co-pay,
deductible and any allowable amount not paid by my carrier, and/or treatment my policy does not cover. I understand that you do accept assignment on
Medicare and 1 will not be responsible for any disallowed charges. However, I understand that 1 am responsible for deductible, co-pay and any charges not
covered by Medicare.
If Tam here as the result of a liability claim, I understand that SPECIALIST HOSPITAL SHREVEPORT cannot wait for settlement of my claim in order to be
paid, and that payment is due at the time services are rendered, My attorney and/or insurance carrier will be provided with an itemized statement for my
reimbursement.
if] am here as the result of an on the job injury and my workman’s comp claim is denied, 1 understand that I am personally responsible for payment of the bilt
in full.
In the event that credit is extended to me, I understand that any bill rendered by SPECLALIST HOSPITAL SHREVEPORT is due and payable upon receipt of
statement. If payment in full creates a financial hardship, the hospital will consider an extended payment plan arrangement. J also understand that J may pay
any or all of my bill in full at any time by credit card (MASTERCARD, VISA, or DISCOVER). In event of default in the payment of any amount due and
this account is placed in the hands of an agency or attorney for collection or legal action, I understand that I will be held liable for my outstanding balance, plus
all court costs and attorney fees incurred as a result. I, the undersigned, have read and understand this contract, and hereby agree to the terms herein.
nee nnOLOLOLOLLOLOLDDN Eee
THE BALANCE BILLING DISCLOSURE NOTICE
a iri ih) ls eel
Professional services rendered by independent healthcare professionals are not part of the hospital, ASC, In-patient Hospice, SNF, or ARCP bill. These
services will be billed to the patient separately. Please understand that physicians or other healthcare professionals may be called upon to provide care or
services to you or on your behalf, but you may not actually see, or be examined by, all physicians or healthcare professionals participating in your care; for
example, you may not see physicians providing radiology, pathology, and EKG interpretation. In many instances, there will be a separate charge for
professional services rendered by physicians to you or on your behalf, and you will receive a bill for these professional services that is separate from the bill for
hospital, ASC, In-patient Hospice, SNF, or ARCP services. These independent healthcare professionals may not participate in your health plan and you may be
responsible for payment of all or part of the fees for the services provided by these physicians who have provided out-of-network services, in addition to
applicable amounts due for copayments, coinsurance, deductibles, and non-covered services.
We encourage you to contact your health plan to determine whether the independent healthcare professionals are participating with your health plan. In order to
obtain the most accurate and up-to-date information about in-network and out-of-network independent healthcare professionals, please contact the customer
service number of your health plan or visit its website. Your health plan is the primary source of information on its provider network and benefits. To help you
determine whether the independent healthcare professionals who provide services at this facility are participating with your health plan, this healthcare facility
has provided you with a complete list of the names and contact information for each individual or group.

SPECIALISTS HOSPITAL SHREVEPORT
A PATIENT’S BILL OF RIGHTS

1. The patient has the right to considerate and respectful care without discrimination due to
age, race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, diagnosis, sexual orientation, or
source of payment.

2. The patient has the right to expect that within its capacity, the hospital must make
reasonable response to the request of a patient for services.

3. The patient has the right to receive, as soon as possible, the services of a translator or
interpreter to facilitate communication between the patient, physician and hospital
healthcare personnel.

4. The patient has the right to obtain from his physician complete and current information
concerning his/her diagnosis, treatment and prognosis in terms the patient can be
reasonably expected to understand. The patient is responsible for asking questions when
they do not understand what they have been told about their care or what they are
expected to do.

5. The patient has the right to receive from his physician information necessary to give
informed consent prior to the start of any procedure and/or treatment. Where medically
significant alternatives for care or treatment exist, the patient has the right to such
information and to know the name of the person responsible for the procedures and/or
treatment. These people shall identify themselves by introduction and/or by wearing a
name tag.

6. The patient has the right to participate in the development and implementation of his/her
plan of care. |

7. The patient has the right to refuse treatment to the extent permitted by law, and to be
informed of the medical consequences of his/her action.

8. The patient has the right to every consideration of his/her privacy concerning his/her own
medical care program.

9. The patient has the right to expect that all communications and records, including all
computerized medical information pertaining to his/her care should be treated as
confidential.

10. The patient has the right to access information contained in his/her medical records
within a reasonable time frame.

11. The patient has the right to obtain information as to any relationship of his/her medical
facility to other health care and educational institutions insofar as his/her care is

13. The patient has the right to appropriate pain assessment and management preoperatively

14. The patient has the right to expect reasonable continuity of care.

necessary or are used as a means of coercion, discipline, convenience or retaliation by
staff.

17. The patient has the right to be free from all forms of abuse and harassment and to receive
care in a safe setting.

18. The patient has the right to be informed by the attending physician and other providers of

from the hospital. The patient shall also have the right to receive assistance from the
physician and appropriate hospital staff in arranging for required follow-up care after
discharge.

19. The patient has the right to formulate advance directives and have hospital staff and |
practitioners who provide care in the hospital comply with these directives.

20. The patient has the right to examine and receive an explanation of his/her hospital bill
regardless of the source of payment and may receive upon request, information relating to
financial assistance available through the hospital.

21. The patient has the right to know what hospital rules and regulations apply to his/her
conduct as a patient. The most important of these is to provide to the best of their
knowledge, accurate and complete medical information about their allergies, present
complaints, past illnesses, hospitalizations, medications and other matters relating to their
